name: Yahoo Finance
description: Free, no-auth market data — stocks, ETFs, indices, currencies, and crypto. Backed by Yahoo's public chart endpoint, the same source the `yfinance` Python library uses. Use for stock quotes, historical OHLCV bars, and symbol search. Note: Yahoo's TOS prohibits unauthorized commercial use and they reserve the right to throttle or change the response shape at any time — best for development, demos, and consumer-grade workloads. For production-grade SLA-backed data, bind a paid provider (Alpaca Market Data, Polygon, IEX).

tools.name: stock_chart
tools.description: Get OHLCV bars + meta (current price, prev close, day range, market state) for a single symbol over a configurable range. Returns timestamps, open/high/low/close/volume arrays. Use for charting, historical analysis, or as a one-shot 'real-time quote' (the meta.regularMarketPrice carries the latest price). Range + interval combinations: 1d/5m, 5d/30m, 1mo/1h, 3mo/1d, 1y/1d, 5y/1wk, max/1mo.

symbol.description: Ticker (AAPL, MSFT, ^GSPC for S&P500, BTC-USD, EURUSD=X).

tools.name: stock_search
tools.description: Search Yahoo's symbol directory by free-text query. Returns matching tickers with name + exchange + asset class. Use to resolve 'apple' → AAPL, 'sp500' → ^GSPC, 'btc' → BTC-USD, etc., before calling stock_chart.

q.description: Free-text query — company name or partial ticker.
